title: Dhumnath Temple
tagline: Dhumnath Temple — spiritual town of Chhattisgarh

overview.short: Dhumnath Temple (also known as Dhoomnath, Dhumesvari, or Dhoomeshwari) is a Shiva temple located in Sargaon village, in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh.
overview.description: Dhumnath Temple (also known as Dhoomnath, Dhumesvari, or Dhoomeshwari) is a Shiva temple located in Sargaon village, in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh. It is listed as a state protected monument and was built during the reign of the Kalachuris of Ratanpura. The temple is situated in the middle of the village, with several houses encroaching upon the hall and ante-chamber towards the east. The eastern wall of the sanctum is incorporated as a courtyard wall in one of the surrounding houses.

topPlaces.name: Sargaon
topPlaces.description: Sargaon is a town and nagar panchayat in the Mungeli district of Chhattisgarh state in India. It is also a Tehsil headquarters and a tribal region.

topPlaces.name: Tala, Chhattisgarh
topPlaces.description: Tala is a village in Bilaspur district, of the Chhattisgarh state of India. It is known for Devrani and Jethani temples and a unique Rudra Shiva stone statue.

topPlaces.name: Maniyari River
topPlaces.description: The Maniyari River is a tributary of the Shivnath River, located in the state of Chhattisgarh, India. It serves as a significant water source for agricultural and domestic purposes in the region.

topPlaces.name: Baitalpur
topPlaces.description: Baitalpur is a town in Mungeli district, Chhattisgarh, India. A historic place since 1897, still serving and providing medical treatment for people affected by leprosy.

topPlaces.name: Bilha, India
topPlaces.description: Bilha is a city and a nagar panchayat in Bilaspur district in the state of Chhattisgarh, India.

items.note: Sargaon is a town and nagar panchayat in the Mungeli district of Chhattisgarh state in India. It is also a Tehsil headquarters and a tribal region.
Tala is a village in Bilaspur district, of the Chhattisgarh state of India. It is known for Devrani and Jethani temples and a unique Rudra Shiva stone statue.
The Maniyari River is a tributary of the Shivnath River, located in the state of Chhattisgarh, India. It serves as a significant water source for agricultural and domestic purposes in the region.
Baitalpur is a town in Mungeli district, Chhattisgarh, India. A historic place since 1897, still serving and providing medical treatment for people affected by leprosy.
Bilha is a city and a nagar panchayat in Bilaspur district in the state of Chhattisgarh, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dhumnath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dhumnath Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dhumnath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (84 km) and the nearest railway station is Bilaspur Junction (26 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dhumnath Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dhumnath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dhumnath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Dhumnath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dhumnath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sargaon, Tala, Chhattisgarh, Maniyari River, Baitalpur, Bilha, India.
